result.meeting_context: Regular meeting of the Downtown Committee (Nov 1) covering approval of minutes, upcoming agenda planning, development and policy updates, Castro Street pedestrian mall transition, vacancy analysis, public safety efforts, community events, and meeting logistics. The committee also adopted the AB 361 remote meeting resolution and the 2023 meeting schedule.

points.speaker: Mike Kasperzak
points.text: The committee approved the October meeting minutes by roll call vote, with no questions or comments raised during discussion, establishing the prior meeting’s record as official.
points.source: Mike Kasperzak : And it sounds like the minutes are approved.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Busking and music in downtown will be addressed in the new year (not this fiscal year); a general maintenance and cleanliness update is planned for the next meeting; and the Castro Bikeway feasibility study is scheduled for February.
points.source: John Lang : We'll be tackling busking and music actually in the new year... General maintenance and cleanliness. I am hoping to have an update for the committee next meeting and then... the bikeway feasibility is scheduled for February.

points.speaker: Marina Marina Keith
points.text: A hot dog vendor dumped cooking oil into a storm drain near Castro Street; her business recorded video sent to the fire department for investigation, and she will share the footage with Eric Cormier for follow-up.
points.source: Marina Marina Keith : ...they're dumping their oil into the storm drain... We have it on camera and we forwarded it to the fire department... We're going to forward them the videos...

points.speaker: Edgar Maravilla
points.text: A new restaurant, Limon, is planned at 800 California Street (intersection of California and Castro). Tenant improvement permits were submitted to the Building Division and are in plan check.
points.source: Edgar Maravilla : ...800 California St... It's a new restaurant going in there... The name of that restaurant is Limon... they submitted a building tenant improvement... going through the plan check...

points.speaker: Edgar Maravilla
points.text: Facade updates have been submitted for 372 Castro Street (law offices building) and a corner building spanning addresses 707–737, with proposed substantial exterior design improvements under review.
points.source: Edgar Maravilla : ...a facade update for 372 Castro... and another facade update... at 707 through 7, 137... that's... substantial improvements as far as the design and the facade...

points.speaker: Edgar Maravilla
points.text: Staff will present an ordinance to the Environmental Planning Commission to cap downtown office space, responding to AB 2097’s removal of parking minimums in January; if recommended, it goes to Council and could remain in effect for up to two years.
points.source: Edgar Maravilla : ...taking an ordinance... to the Environmental Planning Commission to... put a cap on office space... in response to... AB 2097... and... would be... in place for up to two years to 2024.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: For the Robert Green Hope Street hotel, the planning entitlement expires November 27; without a building entitlement by then, the applicant must refile. Maravilla added the proposed office cap won’t affect entitled projects, though a resubmittal could face Precise Plan updates.
points.source: John Lang : ...planning entitlement does expire November 27th. If they don't secure their building entitlement, they will need to refile... / Edgar Maravilla : ...this new proposed cap on office space would not impact projects that are entitled... they may get caught up in the downtown Precise Plan phase one update...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Council adopted the pedestrian mall ordinance for the 100–300 blocks of Castro Street on October 25; it takes effect in 30 days. No claims or objections were filed at the October 11 hearing.
points.source: John Lang : ...Council did adopt an ordinance to enact the pedestrian malls... the ordinance becomes effective within 30 days... So effectively the pedestrian mall is now approved... / John Lang : Were no claims or objections filed...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Final adoption of the 2023 Business Improvement Assessment District is scheduled for November 15, following the initial noticing meeting in October.
points.source: John Lang : We will be having the second reading of the Business Improvement Assessment District for November 15th... this is the second reading final adoption...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Because design guidelines and a functional plan are not yet ready, staff is asking Council (on consent) to extend the Castro Streets program through 2023 to allow continued operations during the transition, after which the program will be rescinded.
points.source: John Lang : ...they will be taking an action... to extend the Castor Streets program... keep Castro Streets in place through 2023... transition period... bring forward design guidelines and a functional plan... then we will rescind the Castro Streets program...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff is working to declutter Castro Street by removing tents and coordinating with businesses to collect broken or worn items as part of ongoing cleaning activities.
points.source: John Lang : ...decluttering Castro a little bit. We've meant tents going away. We've been working with businesses to pick up some of the items... So we're trying to do some cleaning activity...

topics.topic: Vacancy analysis and potential policy tools

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: An inventory found 19 ground-floor vacancies on Castro Street, with three in permitting, two in ownership change, and two with letters of interest, implying 12 likely to remain. Ten non-ground-floor vacancies were counted (an undercount); the 400 block has the most vacancies.
points.source: John Lang : ...of the ground floor vacancies, there's 19... three... going through permit processes... two... changing ownership... two business leads... the ground floor vacancies... end up being 12... on the non ground floor vacancies there's ten... the 400 block we have the most vacancies...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Council will reopen the Downtown Precise Plan to review allowed uses and is considering ‘carrots and sticks’ such as vacancy registration or a vacancy tax, as part of the economic vitality strategy to address long-term vacancies.
points.source: John Lang : ...the Precise plan is going to be opened up again... ability to implement policies to maybe incentivize property owners... carrots and sticks... as part of the economic vitality strategy is a vacancy tax or some vacancy registration.

topics.topic: Retail support and incentives agenda item

points.speaker: Mike Kasperzak
points.text: After discussion about high tenant improvement costs and retail attrition, the chair directed staff to add a future agenda item on economic incentives, including possible retail grant programs, to encourage brick‑and‑mortar retail downtown.
points.source: Mike Kasperzak : ...perhaps John, to add to the sort of list of things is a discussion of possible economic incentives including but not limited to like retail grant programs... we'll put that on the list...

points.speaker: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci
points.text: The downtown tree lighting is Monday, December 5 at 5:30 PM, with the stage moved onto Castro at Mercy to improve viewing. Castro will close from California to Church (maintaining Kaiser access); 'Holidays Around the World' and community performances will return.
points.source: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci : ...tree lighting downtown... Monday, December 5th, 530 start... move the stage onto Castro Street... Castro and Mercy... closing off Castro Street... from California to church, but then allowing for the Kaiser drive through... holidays around the world... community performances...

points.speaker: Kira Pascoe
points.text: Retailers and service businesses are engaging to tell their stories during events, and the DBA will encourage extended hours and promotions around the tree lighting to drive dining and shopping before and after the ceremony.
points.source: Kira Pascoe : ...the merchants who are coming... It is retailers and services... tell the community your story... / Kira Pascoe : ...downtown businesses are planning to extend hours or have things happening as well...

points.speaker: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci
points.text: Holiday décor will include lighted elements in the 400–500 block medians and wreaths on alley archways (with past theft limiting replacements). Street tree lights are currently on and intended to remain on; no holiday pole banners are planned.
points.source: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci : ...light up Penguins... 400 and 500 block... large wreaths... those alleyways... quite a few of them were stolen... / John Lang : They're, they're on. / Maureen Grzn-Pieracci : ...no [holiday] Pennant banners... over the 10,000 mark... bid process...

points.speaker: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci
points.text: 2023 recreation plans include Arbor Day on March 11 at Pioneer Park with interactive elements; KidStock on third Saturdays May–July; Concerts on the Plaza every Friday June–September (larger bands first Fridays); summer outdoor movie nights; National Night Out potentially downtown; and the biannual Multicultural Festival returning in 2024.
points.source: Maureen Grzn-Pieracci : ...return of Arbor Day... March 11th in Pioneer Park... interactive... KidStock... 3rd Saturday... May, June and July... Concerts on the Plaza... every Friday, June through September... summer outdoor movie night series... National Night Out returning potentially downtown... biannual... scheduled... 2024...

points.speaker: Kira Pascoe
points.text: Upcoming DBA/community events include a Meet the Business Owners night at Monte Carlo on November 2 (6–8 PM) and the Taste of Mountain View on November 9, which has slower ticket sales than last time; holiday promotions like a Santa‑thon and 'Elf on a Shelf' are planned.
points.source: Marina Keith : ...at the Monte Carlo... from 6:00 to 8:00 tomorrow night. / Kira Pascoe : ...the taste of Mountain View... Sales are not quite as high as they were... we are not sold out quite yet... / Kira Pascoe : ...looking at some kind of Santa thon... Elf on a Shelf...

points.speaker: Pamela Baird
points.text: The Mountain View Historical Association will conduct a downtown walking tour on Sunday, November 27 at 2:00 PM, meeting in front of the train station; there will be no tour in December.
points.source: Pamela Baird : ...walking tour on Sunday, November 27th at 2:00 PM and meet in front of the train station... we will not have a tour in December.

points.speaker: Matthew Atkins
points.text: The volunteer ambassador program, enhanced lighting, and landscaping changes produced a significant decrease in auto burglaries and overall downtown crime. MVPD will staff officers, CSOs, and volunteers during the holiday season to maintain high visibility and deter crime.
points.source: Matthew Atkins : ...we've seen an uptick of auto burglaries... ambassador program... What we saw was a pretty drastic decrease in auto burglaries... and... overall crime... we are... going into the holiday season... staffing officers and Csos and... volunteers...

points.speaker: Matthew Atkins
points.text: MVPD’s approach to unhoused individuals uses a Community Service Outreach Officer and a Behavioral Services Unit to connect people with services and housing; the public is encouraged to report non-emergency concerns via the police non-emergency line or online reporting.
points.source: Matthew Atkins : ...Community Service Outreach Officer... Officer McPherson... with our Behavioral Service Unit... identify... substance abuse or mental health... get them the services as well as housing... report that... non-emergency line... go online and just do a report...

topics.topic: Traffic signal reactivation and safety outreach

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Villa Street signals are being reactivated in phases with posted signage and monitoring by transportation engineers, due to learned pedestrian behavior and some near misses. The long-term goal is signalized intersections now, with eventual mid-block crossings within pedestrian mall sections in future years.
points.source: John Lang : ...we have been putting signage up... phased approach... traffic engineers out there monitoring... there were some near misses... long term goal is to have it be a somewhat of a signalized intersection... long long term goal... people would actually be crossing in the middle of the street...

points.speaker: Mike Kasperzak
points.text: The committee readopted the AB 361 resolution to continue meeting virtually; staff noted Council may return to in-person meetings early next year, with hybrid formats under consideration, and the committee intends to meet virtually as long as permitted.
points.source: Mike Kasperzak : ...we do... need to re adopt the resolution... we still need to re adopt the resolution... / Mike Kasperzak : ...that motion passes... / John Lang : ...Council would be going back in person at the beginning of the year... considering hybrid meetings...

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: The committee adopted the 2023 schedule: first Tuesdays at 10:00 AM on Feb 7, Mar 7, Apr 4, May 2, Jun 6, Sep 5, Oct 3, Nov 7, and Dec 5, with no meetings in Jan, Jul, or Aug; Lang will confirm virtual meeting allowances and amend if needed.
points.source: John Lang : ...meeting dates would be February 7th, March 7th, April 4th, May 2nd, June 6th, September 5th, October 3rd, November 7th and December 5th... / John Lang : ...happy to follow up with the clerk and bring this back at the next committee if we need to amend... / Mike Kasperzak : ...We got our orders for next... year.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Member Gupta is expected to voluntarily resign due to time conflicts after multiple absences; Council will hold interviews on November 21 to fill vacancies and reappointments.
points.source: John Lang : ...in contact with Mr. Gupta... looks like it will be a voluntary resignation... hope is to get a new appointment... reappointment... / Mike Kasperzak : November. 21st.

points.speaker: Mike Kasperzak
points.text: The chair encouraged participation in upcoming events and extended early Thanksgiving wishes before adjourning the meeting until December.
points.source: Mike Kasperzak : ...we will stand adjourned until December... hope you all have a happy Thanksgiving... And we are adjourned.

decisions.text: John Lang will add a future agenda item on economic incentives, including possible retail grant programs, to support downtown retail.
decisions.source: Mike Kasperzak : ...add to the sort of list of things is a discussion of possible economic incentives including... retail grant programs... we'll put that on the list...

decisions.text: Edgar Maravilla will provide an update on the business status at 845 Villa Street via John Lang to the committee.
decisions.source: Edgar Maravilla : ...I can definitely provide an update to John and he can send it out to everyone...

decisions.text: MVPD will staff officers, CSOs, and volunteers in the downtown area during the holiday season.
decisions.source: Matthew Atkins : ...going into the holiday season is... staffing officers and Csos and... volunteers... going forward...

decisions.text: John Lang will consult the City Clerk and Attorney on virtual meeting allowances and return next meeting with any needed schedule amendments.
decisions.source: John Lang : ...happy to follow up with the clerk and bring this back at the next committee if we need to amend...
